    Home › Manufacturers › Ilyushin

    Ilyushin

    Russia · Founded 1933

    Heavy transports and long-haul airliners built to work anywhere.

    Aircraft here4
    Longest63.94 m
    Combined built1,391
    First flights1963–1988
    The company

    The story so far

    Ilyushin's aircraft were designed for a country of vast distances and rough airfields, and it shows in every one of them: robust landing gear, high wings, ramps, and a tolerance for conditions that would ground more delicate machines.

    The Il-62 gave the Soviet Union its first intercontinental jet, the Il-86 its first widebody, and the Il-96 its four-engine flagship. The Il-76, first flown in 1971 and still in production today, is the most-produced heavy airlifter in history — the workhorse that shows up wherever something big has to be moved.

    The line-up

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
